claims.evidence_text: a 96% relative yield in wheat can be obtained by maintaining an NNI value of 0.93 at the jointing stage by increasing the N dose from 120 to 180 kg N ha −1
claims.claim_summary: Optimal N dose for spring wheat (cv HD-3086) to achieve 96% relative yield is 180 kg N/ha (raised from 120), keeping NNI 0.93 at jointing.

claims.evidence_text: a 96% relative yield in wheat can be obtained by maintaining an NNI value of 0.93 at the jointing stage by increasing the N dose from 120 to 180 kg N ha −1
claims.claim_summary: In-season N management based on NNI/CNDC; N applied at the jointing stage to maintain target nitrogen nutrition index.

claims.evidence_text: the CNDC(G) developed in this study is: Nc = 3.74W −0.431 for a biomass range of 0.96–10.57 tons ha −1
claims.claim_summary: Critical N dilution curve for spring wheat: Nc = 3.74 W^-0.431 (%N vs aboveground biomass W in t/ha) for biomass 0.96-10.57 t/ha.

claims.evidence_text: Nc = 3.74W −0.431 for a biomass range of 0.96–10.57 tons ha −1
claims.claim_summary: Aboveground biomass range over which the critical N dilution curve applies for spring wheat: 0.96-10.57 t/ha.
